events.title: Snap Circuits for Kids
events.description: For ages 8-12. Light up your imagination! In this hands-on workshop, young engineers will explore the basics of electricity and electronics using Snap Circuits. We'll learn how to build simple circuits to power lights, make sounds, and more. Participants will work in pairs to construct projects from the Snap Circuits blueprints. No prior experience is needed—just bring your curiosity! Drop in. No registration required, but space is limited.

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. Please meet at the entrance to the TD Gallery.  Inf

events.title: Cardboard Construction with Makedo
events.description: Create, build, and invent with Makedo cardboard construction tools! Kids will use recycled cardboard and safe building tools to design unique structures and imaginative creations while exploring creativity and basic engineering concepts. Ages 6-12. Registration required.

events.title: Story Time & Craft
events.description: Join us for a fun and engaging storytime followed by a hands-on craft activity perfect for children ages 3-6 with their parents or caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: BenTastic Magic Show
events.description: Find your "inner magic" during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience with BenTastic! Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as he leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "real magicians" during this fast-paced, fun and amazing magic show! For ages 0-12.

events.title: Sunday Family Crafts
events.description: Come as a family and enjoy making crafts together. This program is for families or caregivers with children. Drop-in. No registration required.

events.title: Retro Movie: Jumanji
events.description: Two youngsters play a board game that has real life consequences. Rated PG. Ages 7-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Movie Monday: Dog Man (2025)
events.description: Join us to watch the film and enjoy making a themed craft or colouring. Based on the beloved book series by Dav Pilkey, Dog Man follows the adventures of a half-dog, half-man crimefighter who is sworn to protect and serve. As he doggedly pursues the mischievous feline supervillain, Petey the Cat, chaos, laughter, and heroic antics ensue in this fun-filled animated adventure for the whole family. Age Rating: PG Run Time: 1h 29m For Families. Drop in.

events.title: TD Summer Reading Club Wrap-Up Party!
events.description: Celebrate the end of summer with a party! Enjoy fun games and activities, and enter a draw for a chance to win a small prize. Children ages 12 and under are welcome.  Larger groups are encouraged to call the branch in advance. Drop-in, registration is not required.

events.title: Summer Origami
events.description: Join us for an hour of origami on Monday afternoons. You will learn something new every week and can take home your creations. For children ages 4 and up. Drop in. No registration is required. Daycares and Camps, please call 416-393-7720 to inquire.

events.title: Fun with Snap Circuits
events.description: Play with electricity and circuitry during this hands-on program! Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Libraries Act, sections 20(a), 20(c), and 20(d). events.title: Kids Card Games Club
events.description: Ready to play your cards right? Join us for a different card game each week! Say UNO to boredom, don't go Crazy For Eights, make a splash as you Go Fish for fun, and see if you have what it takes to win the ultimate Game of War. Shuffle the deck and deal yourself into some summer fun!

events.title: Parent/Child Book Club
events.description: Calling all children ages 9 to 12 and their mother, father, grandmother, grandfather, or caregiver! Join our monthly family book club. One child 9 to 12, one parent/caregiver, one book per month. Last Monday of every month, unless noted otherwise. First meeting is on Monday August 31. Registration is required. Please register ONCE for your pair (child and caregiver). Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public L

events.title: Movies @ Albert Campbell Library
events.description: Join us at the library this summer and watch a movie on the big screen! Bring a friend, and a snack! This program is suitable for all ages; younger children must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Drop in. September 1 - The Cat in the Hat (2003). Run Time: 1h 22 min. Rated PG September 4 - Ratatouille (2007). Run Time 1h 51 min. Rated G.

events.title: SNAP Circuits
events.description: Join us for a fun afternoon of Snap circuits! Explore the world of electricity and circuitry during this hands-on program! For children ages 6-12. Parents are welcome to stay during the program, but please only register children. Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Libraries Act, sections 20(a), 20(c), and 20(d). The information will only be used for event and program registration, delivery and evaluatio

events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! For children ages 6-12. Parents are welcome to stay during the program, but please only register children. Informatio

events.title: Summer Carnival
events.description: Celebrate the close of Summer Wonder with a carnival filled with games, activities, and lots of fun! Ages 4-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.

events.title: Mixed Media Forest Adventure for Kids
events.description: Children will explore the beauty of the forest through a fun and imaginative mixed media art program inspired by forest animals and trees. Using a variety of materials such as paint, collage, textured paper, natural elements, and drawing tools, kids will create unique artworks while learning about woodland creatures and nature This is a Drop-In program. No registration required. Space is limited. All materials are provided

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.description: Join us for a summer party with games, treats, and prizes! Weather permitting, we will celebrate outdoors.  For children of all ages. Drop-in. Light snacks will be served at this program. Please advise staff if your child has any allergies.

events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.description: Join us for a hands-on workshop where teens will learn how to make the popular Asian drink Brown Sugar Milk with Boba. This is the 2020 Disney version of the film starring Colin Firth.  Everyone is welcome to this inclusive screening. Drop-in! No registration required.

events.description: This hands-on workshop series introduces the process of building a gaming desktop, from selecting components to installing an operating system. May 28: Star Trek (2009, PG) June 4: Crazy Stupid Love (2001, PG) June 11: The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014, 14A) June 18: Best in Show (2000, PG) June 25: The Big Lebowski (1998, 14A) All are welcome. events.title: Before the Blue Jays: Professional Baseball in Toronto Prior to 1977
events.description: Harvey Sahker presents his book, " Before the Blue Jays " (Dundurn, 2026).​ Discover the fascinating history of professional baseball in Toronto before 1977, when the Blue Jays first took flight. Learn about the major events, venues, and people who trail-blazed the way to the arrival of Major League Baseball in Toronto.

events.title: The A(i), B, C’s of Artificial Intelligence
events.description: This week-long workshop series introduces teens to the four "must knows" of Artificial Intelligence. Through video clips, props, an original AI literacy song, discussions, and hands-on activities, participants will explore how AI works, how it’s used, and how to think critically about its impact. By the end of the program, attendees will be better equipped to navigate AI and social media with awareness and creativity. The workshop is led by Keith McDonald, founder and Chief Curator of Toron

events.title: Beginner Ukulele Workshop
events.description: Have fun learning a new instrument - the ukulele! This workshop will cover fundamentals of ukulele playing: the parts of a ukulele, tuning, strumming patterns, and some basic chords. By the end of this class, you'll have learned the skills you need to start playing your first songs on the ukulele! Beginner friendly, no previous experience is necessary. For ages 7 to 17. events.title: Beginner's Guide to Making Comics
events.description: Have you ever wanted to create your own comic, manga, or anime-inspired story? In this interactive workshop, teens will learn the basics of comic creation through hands-on activities, creative storytelling, and guided drawing exercises. Participants will explore comic and manga techniques including panels, page layouts, speech bubbles, sound effects, character design, and visual storytelling. They’ll also learn how to build engaging characters and create their own one-page comic or manga scene.

events.title: Youth Hub: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Saturday Knit & Crochet Circle
events.description: Want to learn how to knit or crochet with a fun group of people? Looking for help with your project? You can also share your projects with others, teach or learn a technique, and enjoy great conversation. All levels of expertise are welcome. Bring your own yarn, needles, and crochet hooks. All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.title: Youth Hub: Finding a Job with ACCES Employment
events.description: Workplace Readiness & Soft Skill This session focuses on essential workplace skills such as communication, teamwork, time management, and professionalism. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available.&nbs

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: LivingWorks SafeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks SafeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks SafeTALK certificat

events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.

events.description: Come and join us on the third Tuesday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! This year we are reading:  June 23 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 15 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 17 -  Small Things Like These by Claire Keegan
